one_time_description: Correction of an error: amounts received from BC Housing by Katzie First Nation Housing Society were incorrectly recorded as revenue instead of long-term debt in prior year
economic_notes: Katzie Development Corporation Ltd. and Katzie Development Limited Partnership accounted for under modified equity method. Nation operates Katzie First Nation Bus Corporation, Smuqwa Development Corporation, Katzie Cultural Education Society, Katzie Early Years Society, Katzie First Nation Housing Society, and Katzie First Nation Affordable Housing Development Society. COVID-19 impacts noted including remote work and emergency response procedures.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Comparative 2020 figures are restated due to correction of an error where BC Housing funds received by Katzie First Nation Housing Society were incorrectly recorded as revenue instead of long-term debt ($540,332). The Nation has 6 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 2 GBEs (Katzie Development Corporation Ltd. and Katzie Development Limited Partnership) accounted for under the modified equity method. Foreign exchange loss of $532,569 recorded as other expense. Government of Canada budget was $412,574 but actual was $0. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,684,960 + Province of BC $3,393,291 + FNHA $899,872 + CMHC $93,303 + CMHC Supplemental $92,746 + BC Treaty Commission $550,000 + FNLMRC $423,285 + WWF $250,714 + Surrey Schools $65,428 + First Peoples Heritage $33,700 + SASET $22,729 + DFO $20,289 = $9,529,317; however segmented data shows other government revenue of $4,668,633 plus ISC $3,684,960 = $8,353,593 which may be more accurate as some items like BC Treaty Commission and WWF may be classified differently. OSR calculated as total revenue minus government transfers per segment disclosure.
